goal: Fix file drilldown scrollbar visibility and eliminate high CPU usage (100%) during idle states.
constraints: Beta readiness standards must be maintained (lint, tests passing).
Avoid regressing existing functionality (e.g., first scan, cancellation).
Minimize impact on user experience (no visible UI glitches).
done: Identified root cause of idle CPU spike: FSEventsWatcher triggering RecentChangeService -> DB -> full working set replacement repeatedly.
Disabled automatic file watcher (`enableAutomaticFileWatcher = false`) to stop background processing while idle.
Fixed drilldown scrollbar visibility issue by enhancing `HiddenScrollIndicators` to recursively hide scrollbars in the window hierarchy and trigger updates on layout changes.
Implemented lazy teardown of the panel's NSHostingView when the popover closes to eliminate SwiftUI display cycle overhead while hidden.
Verified idle CPU usage dropped from ~100% to ~0.01% and RSS stabilized around 50MB.
Committed fixes: `fix: stabilize beta scan readiness` (6f116d5) and subsequent patches for idle/scrollbar issues.
in_progress: Final validation of the scrollbar fix in the UI (build successful, awaiting manual verification).
next_steps: Manually verify that the scrollbar is hidden in the file drilldown view.
Run overnight/runtime monitor to ensure low CPU usage persists.
Update issue #9 (CPU 150% when app runs for extended periods) with findings and resolution.
Address issue #18 (Release artifact metadata) if the current build is to be released.
Update Beta checklist (issue #11) with final status.
